rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current component or its subcomponents when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped and do not introduce new identifiers that conflict with existing AADL elements
Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks, ensuring that the structure and connections remain intact
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on whether they refer to component ports or implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Avoid placing assumptions or guarantees in the wrong scope, which can lead to unresolved references or type mismatches
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are properly represented through valid variables or outputs
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks do not introduce new identifiers that conflict with existing AADL elements
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
